interactions.description: Taking Ibuprofen and Aspirin together increases risk of gastrointestinal bleeding and reduces effectiveness of Aspirin.
Two NSAIDs together significantly increase risk of stomach ulcers, bleeding and kidney damage.
Combining two NSAIDs increases risk of gastrointestinal bleeding and kidney problems.
Combining Paracetamol with alcohol severely increases risk of liver damage.
Alcohol with Metformin increases risk of lactic acidosis — a dangerous condition.
Amoxicillin can increase Methotrexate toxicity leading to serious side effects.
Antacids reduce absorption of Ciprofloxacin. Take Ciprofloxacin 2 hours before or 6 hours after antacids.
Combining Sertraline with Ibuprofen increases risk of bleeding, especially gastrointestinal.
Two SSRIs together can cause Serotonin Syndrome — a potentially life-threatening condition.
Two SSRIs together can cause Serotonin Syndrome — a potentially life-threatening condition.
Combining these can cause excessive lowering of blood pressure and heart rate. Monitor closely.
Ibuprofen reduces effectiveness of Furosemide and can worsen kidney function.
Ibuprofen significantly increases bleeding risk in patients taking Warfarin.
Aspirin and Warfarin together greatly increase risk of serious bleeding.
Can cause excessive lowering of blood pressure. Monitor blood pressure regularly.
Antacids reduce absorption of Levothyroxine. Take Levothyroxine 4 hours apart from antacids.
Antacids may slightly reduce absorption of Nitrofurantoin. Take 2 hours apart.
Antacids severely reduce absorption of Doxycycline. Take at least 2 hours apart.
Generally safe to take together for short periods. However long-term use together may affect kidney function.
Alcohol increases drowsiness caused by Cetirizine. Avoid driving or operating machinery.
Causes severe nausea, vomiting, flushing and rapid heartbeat. Avoid alcohol completely during treatment.
Antacids reduce absorption of Azithromycin. Take 1 hour before or 2 hours after antacids.
Antacids significantly reduce absorption of iron. Take iron 2 hours apart from antacids.
Omeprazole may slightly increase Metformin levels. Generally safe but monitor for Metformin side effects.
